rationale: The page contains detailed technical specifications and developmental details for several Indian and international air defense systems, including IADWS components, Project Kusha, Anant Shastra, and a comparative table of global systems like THAAD and Iron Dome. This provides high-quality factual content for question generation.
cleanedText: Components of IADWS. Command Centre: Integrated operation is controlled by a Centralized Command and Control Centre developed by Defence Research & Development Laboratory (DRDL). Indigenous Quick Reaction Surface to Air Missiles (QRSAM): Developed by DRDO. A short-range SAM system designed to protect moving armoured columns from aerial attacks. Configured on mobile platform. Advanced Very Short-Range Air Defence System (VSHORADS) missiles: Developed by Research Centre Imarat (RCI). Man Portable Air Defence System (MANPADS) designed to neutralize low-altitude aerial threats at short ranges. A high-power laser-based Directed Energy Weapon (DEW): Developed by Centre for High Energy Systems and Sciences. Related News. Project Kusha or Program Long Range Surface to Air missile (PGLRSAM). An indigenous initiative led by DRDO to develop a long-range air defence missile system. Consists of Two Phases: Phase 1: Includes 4 interceptors’ variants (under developments: M1, M2, M3 and Naval variant). Phase 2: Aims to develop an even longer range (exceeding 600 km) interceptor missile. Anant Shastra Air Defence System. Indian Army has awarded a contract to BEL for development and supply of ‘Anant Shastra’ air defense system. About Anant Shastra: An indigenous Quick Reaction Surface-to-Air Missile (QRSAM) system. Developed by: DRDO in collaboration with BEL and Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL). Capabilities: Highly mobile and agile with a range of around 30 km. 6.1.3. OTHER AIR DEFENCE SYSTEM IN NEWS. Iron Dome, David’s Sling, Arrow-2, Arrow-3 (Israel): Iron Dome (short range), David’s Sling (short to medium and medium to long range), Arrow (long range). The Terminal High Altitude Area Defence (THAAD) (U.S.A.): Intercepts ballistic missiles. Golden Dome (U.S.A.): Expected deployment by 2029. Inspired by Israel’s Iron Dome, but it will include both surveillance satellites and interceptor satellites to target enemy missiles. PATRIOT Missile Air Defence System (PMADS) (U.S.A.): PATRIOT (Phased Array Tracking Radar for Intercept on Target) is a mobile surface-to-air missile defence system. Barak Magen (Israel): Barak Magen (the Hebrew name of Lightning Shield) is an advanced naval air defense system. HQ-9 system (China): Inspired by the Russian S-300.
